title: Crataegus petrodavisii
intro: Crataegus petrodavisii, commonly known as Davis's hawthorn or Petrodavis's hawthorn, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antioxidant. The bioactive constituents of Crataegus petrodavisii include flavonoids, such as quercetin and epicatechin, as well as oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) and triterpenoids. Crataegus petrodavisii is often used to make herbal preparations, including tea, capsules, and extracts, which are commonly used to support cardiovascular health and well-being.
